Follow these steps to connect Clawket to: Follow these steps to connect Clawket to your OpenClaw.
Follow these steps to set up or fix your: Follow these steps to set up or fix your connection.
Make sure the OpenClaw process is runnin: Make sure the OpenClaw process is running on your host machine. Try running these commands to verify:
If using the default connection method,: If using the default connection method, make sure your network connection is stable.
If using LAN connection, make sure your: If using LAN connection, make sure your phone and your OpenClaw machine are on the same local network.
The default WebSocket port is 18789. Mak: The default WebSocket port is 18789. Make sure it is not blocked by a firewall or occupied by another process.
Open openclaw.json on the host and confi: Open openclaw.json on the host and confirm the auth token or password matches what you entered in the app.
If the quick connect guide above did not: If the quick connect guide above did not help, try these steps.
This is usually caused by network change: This is usually caused by network changes (Wi-Fi switching, sleep mode). The app will automatically reconnect. If it persists, try restarting the Gateway from Settings.
Double-check the auth token or password: Double-check the auth token or password in your connection settings. If you recently regenerated credentials, update them in the app.
Update OpenClaw on your host with: npm u: Update OpenClaw on your host with: npm update -g @nicepkg/openclaw. Then restart the Gateway.
OpenClaw is an open-source AI gateway th: OpenClaw is an open-source AI gateway that runs on your machine. Clawket is its mobile companion — it connects to your OpenClaw instance so you can chat with AI agents, manage cron jobs, browse files, and monitor usage from your phone.
These examples are based on OpenClaw Gat: These examples are based on OpenClaw Gateway docs and config validation. Use the mode that matches how your phone reaches the Gateway.
Use this when your phone is on the same: Use this when your phone is on the same local network as the OpenClaw host.
Use this when both devices are in the sa: Use this when both devices are in the same tailnet and you are not using tailscale serve.
Direct Tailnet bind does not use Serve o: Direct Tailnet bind does not use Serve or Funnel.
Use this when you want Tailscale to publ: Use this when you want Tailscale to publish HTTPS for the Gateway while OpenClaw stays on loopback.
Tailscale Serve requires gateway.bind to: Tailscale Serve requires gateway.bind to stay on loopback.
gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins is only: gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins is only for browser-based Control UI or WebChat on non-loopback addresses. Clawket app connection itself does not depend on this field.
If you open Control UI over LAN or direc: If you open Control UI over LAN or direct Tailnet bind, add the exact browser origin you open in Safari or Chrome.
If you open Control UI through Tailscale: If you open Control UI through Tailscale Serve, the browser origin is your HTTPS MagicDNS host.
For Tailscale Serve, keep using your Gat: For Tailscale Serve, keep using your Gateway token or password in Clawket. Tailscale identity header auth applies to browser Control UI or WebChat, not app login.
After changing openclaw.json, restart th: After changing openclaw.json, restart the Gateway.

Keep Clawket CLI up to date: Keep Clawket CLI up to date
When using advanced features, make sure: When using advanced features, make sure your Clawket CLI is on the latest version.
If an advanced feature fails, try updati: If an advanced feature fails, try updating the package first. You can copy the command below and ask Agent to run it, or run it manually yourself.
After the update finishes, please run `c: After the update finishes, please run `clawket restart` as well to ensure the new version takes effect.
If you want Agent to handle the whole pr: If you want Agent to handle the whole process, copy the prompt below and send it directly.
Please first upgrade the installed clawk: Please first upgrade the installed clawket client on this computer by running `npm install -g @p697/clawket@latest`, then run `clawket restart` after the installation completes.
Remotely run diagnostics on your OpenCla: Remotely run diagnostics on your OpenClaw instance
Exit now? This will interrupt the curren: Exit now? This will interrupt the current operation.
Diagnostics require a relay connection. Connect via Clawket Bridge to use this feature.
The settings below still write global ex: The settings below still write global exec fields, but they do not directly control whether the current agent can use command tools on this setup.
This writes a global OpenClaw field. It: This writes a global OpenClaw field. It can affect some execution paths, but it does not override the current agent's own tool access.
This will change the current agent's den: This will change the current agent's deny list and restart OpenClaw Gateway. Continue?
Blocked means this agent cannot run comm: Blocked means this agent cannot run commands or manage background processes.
Available means this agent can use comma: Available means this agent can use command tools when its current tool profile allows them.
The settings below are global exec setti: The settings below are global exec settings. The control above is the one that directly changes this agent's command access.
Deny turns command execution off on the: Deny turns command execution off on the paths that follow this global rule.
AllowList only lets through commands tha: AllowList only lets through commands that OpenClaw already trusts.
Full broadly allows commands on the path: Full broadly allows commands on the paths that follow this global rule.
Every Command asks before each command.
Unknown Only asks only when OpenClaw doe: Unknown Only asks only when OpenClaw does not already recognize the command.
Never runs without asking on the paths t: Never runs without asking on the paths that follow this global rule.
OpenClaw will confirm every command befo: OpenClaw will confirm every command before it runs.
